Health & Environment (50)

Endangered data helps find deadly mercury

Ken Ward Jr. May-June 2006
Government & PoliticsHealth & Environment

TRI data was used to investigate mercury pollution from chlor-alkali plants, revealing significant environmental concerns.

Starting small

David Donald September - October 2004
Investigative MethodsHealth & EnvironmentJournalism & Newsroom

Guidance for journalists in small newsrooms to implement computer-assisted reporting by using available resources and starting with manageable projects.

Guiding the project from start to finish

Tim Ragones September - October 2004
Investigative MethodsHealth & EnvironmentJournalism & Newsroom

John Ullmann provides tips for editors on managing computer-assisted reporting projects, highlighting teamwork and storytelling.

SPOTLIGHT: ELECTIONS Using CAR to cover the vote

Megan Clarke June 2004
Investigative MethodsGovernment & PoliticsHealth & Environment

Megan Clarke highlights how journalists are using computer-assisted reporting to uncover deeper insights into the 2004 elections beyond basic campaign finance.

Making the connection between doctors, ODs

Fred Schulte March 2004
Health & EnvironmentData & Databases

A series exposed Florida doctors prescribing large quantities of narcotics to low-income Medicaid users, leading to high financial costs and overdose deaths.

Survey helps explain heart attack survival

Robert Davis March 2004
Health & Environment

An investigation into varying cardiac arrest survival rates across cities highlighted how some save substantially more victims due to differing emergency response capabilities.

Taking the pulse of a body of water

Lisa Stiffler September/October 2003
Health & Environment

A look at the health of Puget Sound using various indicators of pollution, exploring sources and impacts on marine life.

Databases show implant troubles

Robert Gebeloff February 2003
Health & Environment

Analysis of FDA data reveals issues with medical implants and regulatory system flaws.

The daily grind

Neil Reisner May 2000
Data & DatabasesInvestigative MethodsHealth & Environment

Driving computer-assisted reporting requires preparation, akin to practicing for a concert. It involves anticipating and analyzing available data to enhance reporting on deadline.

David Wethe March 2000
Data & DatabasesInvestigative MethodsHealth & Environment

A report on the growth of computer-assisted reporting (CAR) globally, highlighting issues of access to electronic information and successful projects in various countries.

Dave Davis September 1999
Investigative MethodsHealth & Environment

LEGAL DRUG HAZARDS Inside the FDA

Stephen Fried June 1999
Health & Environment

The article is based on a personal investigation into the pharmaceutical industry, uncovering the inner workings of the FDA and exposing the dangers of legal drugs.

DEADLY PRACTICE? Cosmetic Surgery

Fred Schulte June 1999
Government & PoliticsBusiness & EconomyHealth & Environment

An investigation into malpractice in cosmetic surgery offices in Florida, with a focus on poorly trained doctors performing lengthy operations and the resulting fatalities.

CORRECTIONAL CARE: Prisoner health

David Heath December 1998
Investigative MethodsCrime & JusticeHealth & Environment

Investigation into the neglect and inadequate health care provided to prisoners by for-profit health services, exemplified by the death of inmate Calvin Moore.

NURSING HOME NEGLECT: Unraveled care

Mark J. Rochester July 1998
Investigative MethodsHealth & EnvironmentGovernment & Politics

An investigative series uncovered disturbing levels of neglect and regulatory shortcomings in Indianapolis nursing homes, prompting immediate reforms from state officials.

HEALTH CARE IN THE ARMED FORCES: System protecting military malpractice

Stuart Watson May 1998
Investigative MethodsPublic SafetyHealth & Environment

The report uncovers systemic issues in the military health care system that protect negligent practices, highlighting lack of malpractice insurance for military doctors.

"Rocking the Rules: Baby Business"

Joan Mazzolini and Dave Davis December 1996
Health & Environment

An investigation into Ohio hospitals that circumvent rules to treat premature babies, exposing higher death rates in facilities with lower care levels.

Diagnosing Hospital Health

Rick Linsk and Paul D'Ambrosio December 1996
Health & Environment

A series titled 'Vital Signs' investigates the health of New Jersey hospitals, spurring significant discussion and action within the state's medical establishment.

Christopher Schmitt May 1991
Health & Environment

A four-part series reveals numerous flaws in California's toxic waste monitoring system, including unaccounted waste and regulatory failures.
